mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production indicates expanding economic output, which increases corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO growth are not positive in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint forces investors into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in nonfarm payrolls signals contracting labor demand that reduces household income and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises while PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrence of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS does not coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery periods, sustained increases in housing starts signal a rebound in construction activity and household formation, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive momentum in HOUST during post-crisis recovery periods is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in consumer price inflation above 2% annualized over three months erodes real corporate margins and increases the probability of future monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 2% annualized over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: Months with CPIAUCSL accelerating above 2% annualized show positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while the federal funds rate is already below 0.25%, the constraint on conventional monetary easing amplifies recession fears, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Instances of falling INDPRO when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income that supports aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive monthly changes in PAYEMS coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, elevated labor slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with UNRATE above 8.0% exhibit positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months.
